programmes.show_description: From desserts at a historic café to a glass blowing workshop, Jolly discovers all that Rattenberg, Austria's smallest town, has to offer.
When Sukhmani visits the Angkor temples, she is surprised to discover that these were not only shrines but also status symbols.
Heather explores colonial buildings and visits Santa Cruz, ending her day at San Andreas. Next she explores zoological park.
From St. Matthias Church to the Royal Palace, Catriona explores the iconic attractions of Budapest, "the Paris of the East".
Where cobblestone alleys meet disco lights - Robbie lands in Stockholm, a city that grooves to its own beat. The adventure kicks off in style.
Daniel Coll & Paddy Doherty ride in a classic steam locomotive to Hitoyoshi, where they enjoy a relaxing hot spring bath in the mountainside.
Located in the heart of the Austrian Alps' most highly glaciated section, Zillertal offers Jolly the thrills of winter in summer.
Sukhmani visits Siem Reap and is delighted to find the energy of a big city and the charm of a small town.
Upon seeing El Teide, Spain's highest mountain, Heather realizes why people of the ancient Guanche tribe believed that the peak holds up the sky.
From a bustling old Victorian market to the Titanic Belfast museum, Olivia gets to know the different sides of Belfast through the places she visits.
One rookie, one snowstorm, one giant slope - Robbie swaps city life for ski life in the wild winter playground of Kungsberget.
Daniel Coll & Paddy Doherty try bungee jumping at Kobae Bridge. They also explore nature and mythology at Takachiho.
On his way from Zillertal to Innsbruck, Jolly makes some rather fascinating pit-stops that lead him to uncover shades of Austria's heritage.
Longing for an adrenaline rush, Sukhmani goes to Sihanoukville, a popular Cambodian beach retreat, which is famous for its water sports.
From the sand dunes of Maspaloma to the canyons of the Municipality of Santa Lucia, Gran Canaria boasts a rich diversity of landscapes.
From palaces to museums, the buildings in Munich are an intriguing mix of traditional Bavarian architecture and avant-garde design.
Robbie steps into a fairytale on the island of Gotland - medieval walls, mysterious lagoons, and rugged beauty collide in this windswept wonderland.
Daniel Coll & Paddy Doherty try some adventurous activities at Mount Aso, the caldera that is home to Japan's most active volcano.
On the last leg of his action-packed gastronomic escapade across Austria, Chef Jolly arrives at the captivating city of Innsbruck.
Sukhmani loses her heart to the unspoiled beauty of the lesser-explored islands around the beach town of Sihanoukville.
Hannah Jackson visits the Borobudur temple and explores the local art and nightlife scenes in Jogjakarta.
From the world's largest mall to a magical desert safari, the host realizes that Dubai is one of the world's most glamorous destinations.
The home of a great night's sleep. Every body. Every bedroom. Every budget.
From meeting the "Queen of Bangkok Street Food" to eating Pad Thai at the place where it was born, Emeline lives a foodie's dream in Bangkok.
Find out how the flavours of China have come to be such an integral part of the cuisine of Singapore.
The capital of Cambodia, Phnom Penh is littered with monuments that narrate stories of the country's past.
Hannah Jackson visits the youngest volcano on the ancient Tengger caldera, Mount Bromo. She also goes to see the Komodo dragons.
From the fragrances at the Perfume Souk to a hot air balloon ride in the desert, Sabrina gets a taste of heritage and luxury at Madinat Jumeirah.
Holiday Inspiration. Discover the perfect holiday for you, featuring the hottest destinations and exclusive deals to make your next holiday the best one.
At an elephant conservation centre in Lampang, Emeline experiences the immense love that the Thai people feel for their national animal.
From fish head curry to 'Rojak', why do visitors from India in Singapore find it difficult to identify with the Indian dishes in the country?
Jamie Srubis tries out spa treatments, which are inspired by the Dead Sea and the desert surroundings at Hotel Kempinski Ishtar in Jordan.
Hannah Jackson explores the scenery, culture and flavours on the islands of Komodo National Park. She also visits the Taman Ayun temple in Bali.
From the Pyramids of Giza to the Saladin Citadel of Cairo, Egypt's monuments are a testament to the different beliefs that have shaped this country.
On an island-hopping adventure in Mauritius, Krisann and Nathan find out that time works differently on these islands.
While exploring the extraordinary temples in Chiang Rai, Emeline begins to uncover the stories hidden in their fascinating architecture.
From spirituality and nature to food and adventure, Hannah Jackson explores all that Bali has to offer travelers.
The Khajuraho group of temples have awed generations with their architectural brilliance, intricate carvings and their famous erotic sculptures.
Jamie Srubis tries out spa treatments, which are inspired by the Dead Sea and the desert surroundings at Hotel Kempinski Ishtar in Jordan.
Jamie Srubis learns more about Ayurveda through the traditional spa treatments at the Kumarakom Lake Resort in Kerala.
Emeline visits Koh Tao - a small island in the Gulf of Thailand often referred to as a 'diver's paradise'.
What does the cuisine of Singapore, which has almost entirely been shaped by immigrants, tell us about the culture of the land?
From spirituality and nature to food and adventure, Hannah Jackson explores all that Bali has to offer travelers.
The Khajuraho group of temples have awed generations with their architectural brilliance, intricate carvings and their famous erotic sculptures.
A paradise for luxury fans, a mecca for water sports enthusiasts, a wonderland for nature lovers, Maldives is a destination with many sides to it.
Forget the clichés -a these five epic Scottish experiences will shatter everything you thought you knew.
Emeline visits Koh Tao - a small island in the Gulf of Thailand often referred to as a 'diver's paradise'.
What does the cuisine of Singapore, which has almost entirely been shaped by immigrants, tell us about the culture of the land?
Barcelona was the canvas of the world-renowned architect, Antoni Gaudi, who branded the city with his fantastical creations.
From a rich lamb curry to a hearty pulao made with rare mushrooms, Alex learns to make the secret recipes of a local family in Delhi.
From the divine Thikse monastery to the serene Pangong Lake, Alex Outhwaite soaks in the Zen at Leh.
Exploring Brussels makes Catriona realize that it's the city where historic meets hip, eccentric meets futuristic and traditional meets cosmopolitan.
Charlie's journey across his homeland brings him to the birthplace of tequila, the iconic Mexican spirit to understand what makes it irresistible.
While exploring Alexandria's iconic attractions, the host experiences the sharp contrast of history and modernity that characterizes the city.
While checking out the art scene of Madrid and enjoying the liveliness at a local market, Alex is engulfed by the energy of this young European city..
From papad ka paratha to lehsun ki kheer, Chef Aditya Jaimini shows Alex Outhwaite how to make some of the unusual dishes from Indian cuisine.
At Nubra Valley, Alex participates in the local dance, and then sets off for a ride on a Bactrian camel across the Hunder sand dunes.
Cologne is all things romantic from the chocolate museum to its perfume shop. It is a city in love with life, one that knows how to have a good time.
From the towering Peña de Bernal monolith to the Queretaro wine region, Charlie visits some of Mexico's lesser-known cultural gems.
The respect accorded to women in ancient Egypt is evident in almost every aspect of the civilization from the religious beliefs to social customs.
Alex finds a piece of Africa called Bioparc in Spain's third largest city, Valencia.
Alex uncovers the secret behind the irresistible Shahi Dal at Amritsar's iconic eating joint, Kesar Da Dhaba.
From a Sikh Gurudwara to an organization devoted to the betterment of Ladakhi people, Alex finds herself inspired by the humanity in Kargil.
From soothing landscapes to relaxing massages, Indeya Passfield explores all that Kandy has to offer in terms of rejuvenation.
Charlie Rubio visits one of the many 'indigenous paradises' across Mexico, where he gets a chance to visit a hidden heron sanctuary on an island.
Sabrina Chakici marvels at the architectural wonders of Baku, the capital of Azerbaijan, while also checking out souvenirs at the local bazaar.
Be it Europe's largest marine aquarium or a bullfighting ring, Alex finds Spain's modern elegance and old-world charm meeting in Valencia.
The cuisine of Punjab is rich in flavour because it is farm-to-fork and still employs traditional cooking methods such as cooking in a charcoal oven.
A museum in Kargil transports Alex back to the times when travelers and traders traversed the Silk Road and camped in Ladakh.
The host visits Dubai's very own tropical rainforest and a wildlife sanctuary that is home to wild flamingos.
Holiday Inspiration. Discover the perfect holiday for you, featuring the hottest destinations and exclusive deals to make your next holiday the best one.
Sabrina Chakici visits the city of Ganja in Azerbaijan, where she uncovers the history at sights like Heyder Aliyev Park and Mausoleum Nizami.
Jamie Srubis tries out basic Ayurvedic spa treatments such as the Four-Hands Synchronous Massage at the Vasundhara Sarovar Premiere Resort in Kerala.
Alex Outhwaite meets a Nawab in Lucknow, who shows her how to make Gosht Mawazad, a decadent Awadhi dish with mutton and dry fruits.
From stumbling upon a statue of Guru Rinpoche to attending a local festival at a monastery, Alex explores the influence of Buddhism in Zanskar Valley.
From the stories of famous Egyptian pharaohs to Greco-Roman beliefs, the ancient temples of Egypt contain mysteries waiting to unfold.
The home of a great night's sleep. Every body. Every bedroom. Every budget.
In Sheki, Sabrina Chakici learns about the art form, Shabaka, indulges in a crude oil spa treatment and explores Khinalug Village.
Jamie Srubis tries some of the signature spa treatments of the Shangri La Resort at Kota Kinabalu in Sabah, Malaysia.
Alex Outhwaite meets the descendants of the royal families of Hyderabad and Lucknow, and they tell her about the culture and cuisine of both kingdoms.
From a magical shikara ride on Dal Lake to an elaborate Kashmiri Wazwan, Alex experiences "paradise on Earth" in Srinagar.
The central plains of Sri Lanka are dotted with crumbling temples, lost cities and ruins of sacred Buddhist sites are few of the sites now ranking as national treasures.
In Cuatro Ciénegas, Charlie finds himself uncovering the wonders of the destination, one more surreal than the other.
The pre-Mughal Islamic architecture of Champaner and the Rajput temples of Pavagadh have earned them the title, "University of Architecture".
The host comes to know many interesting facts and stories about the pyramids of Giza, when she visits them.
From the land of the hallowed Camp Nou stadium to the Catalan capital boasting amazing seafood, Barcelona is a city with many shades to it.
From the Perperikon fort to the Sofia Central Market, Catriona explores the cultural treasures of Sofia, the capital of Bulgaria.
Travelling along the scenic Manali-Leh highway, Alex stops and camps at Korzok village, where she explores the simple ways of life.
On the map of Mexico, Tlaquepaque looks like just another suburb of Guadalajara, but Charlie knows that there is more to this town than meets the eye.
On a journey to find the lost and forgotten recipes of India, Alex finds herself at Asia's biggest spice market.
The host comes to know many interesting facts and stories about the pyramids of Giza, when she visits them.
From the land of the hallowed Camp Nou stadium to the Catalan capital boasting amazing seafood, Barcelona is a city with many shades to it.
From the Perperikon fort to the Sofia Central Market, Catriona explores the cultural treasures of Sofia, the capital of Bulgaria.
Travelling along the scenic Manali-Leh highway, Alex stops and camps at Korzok village, where she explores the simple ways of life.
On the map of Mexico, Tlaquepaque looks like just another suburb of Guadalajara, but Charlie knows that there is more to this town than meets the eye.
On a journey to find the lost and forgotten recipes of India, Alex finds herself at Asia's biggest spice market.
